message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles, Zap, Star } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Anime Tactical Simulator Legendary Units: Complete Guide & Tier List 2026",
  description: "Master Anime Tactical Simulator legendary units with our 2026 guide. Learn evolution strategies, tower progression, and the best units to keep for your squad.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Legendary+Units+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Anime Tactical Simulator Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Anime Tactical Simulator Legendary Units** serve as your essential early-game foundation and core evolution material.
- **Priority Goal**: Focus on clearing Tower Mode floors 10, 25, 50, and 100 to unlock permanent unit deployment slots.
- **Resource Management**: Never sell Mythic or Legendary units early; you need them for Souls and high-tier evolution.
- **Evolution Meta**: Souls are obtained by selling units of matching rarity, making duplicate Legendaries highly valuable.
- **Progression Tip**: Use active codes immediately to secure Gems and Dungeon Keys for a massive early-game power spike.
</Callout>

In the rapidly evolving landscape of Roblox tower defense, Anime Tactical Simulator has emerged as a standout title in 2026. Success in this game depends entirely on your ability to manage your roster effectively, specifically focusing on **anime tactical simulator legendary units**. While Mythic and Secret units represent the pinnacle of power, Legendary units are the workhorses that bridge the gap between a struggling beginner and a dominant late-game player. Understanding how to summon, upgrade, and eventually evolve these units is the difference between getting stuck in World 1 and breezing through the high-HP challenges of World 3 and beyond. This guide provides a comprehensive breakdown of the current meta, resource management strategies, and the most efficient path to building a top-tier squad.

<Section icon={Star} title="Understanding Unit Rarities and the Meta">

In Anime Tactical Simulator, units are categorized by a distinct rarity system that dictates their base stats, ability scaling, and evolution potential. While it is tempting to chase only the rarest pulls, a well-optimized team of Legendary units often outperforms a poorly managed Mythic team due to easier access to duplicates for evolution.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="Legendary Units">
    - **Rarity**: Purple
    - **Role**: Core damage and early-game carries.
    - **Ease of Use**: High; easy to evolve and find duplicates.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Mythic Units">
    - **Rarity**: Red
    - **Role**: High-tier DPS and essential late-game utility.
    - **Ease of Use**: Moderate; requires significant Soul investment.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Secret Units">
    - **Rarity**: Yellow/Gold
    - **Role**: Game-breaking power and ultimate abilities.
    - **Ease of Use**: Low; extremely rare drops from Tower or events.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

The 2026 meta emphasizes damage-per-second (DPS) and deployment efficiency. Because unit slots are limited, every character on your field must contribute significantly. Legendary units provide the best "bang for your buck" in terms of Yen cost versus damage output during the mid-game transition.

| Rarity | Primary Use | Evolution Difficulty | Recommended Action |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Common** | Fodder/Fusing | N/A | Fuse for EXP or sell for basic Yen |
| **Rare** | Early Fodder | N/A | Fuse into Legendary units |
| **Epic** | Placeholder | Low | Fuse for high EXP gains |
| **Legendary** | Core Squad | Medium | **Keep and Evolve** |
| **Mythic** | Late Game | High | **Always Keep** |

<Callout type="tip" title="Pro Tip">
If you pull a duplicate Legendary unit, do not use it for EXP fusion. Save it for the Evolution pedestal, as you will need exact copies to reach higher power tiers in later worlds.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Zap} title="Active Codes and Early Summoning Strategy">

To jumpstart your collection of **anime tactical simulator legendary units**, you must take advantage of the active code system. The developers frequently release codes that provide Gems, which are the primary currency for summoning new units. In March 2026, several high-value codes are available to help new players skip the initial grind.

| Code | Rewards | Requirement |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **RELEASE** | 500 Gems, 5 Dungeon Keys | None |
| **UPDATE1** | 1000 Gems, 10 Potions | None |
| **DISCORD** | 3 Talent Rerolls, 2 Premium Rerolls | Join Official Group |
| **THANKYOU** | 5 Dungeon Keys, 500 Yen | None |
| **MILESTONE** | 750 Gems, 5 Potions | None |
| **LAUNCH** | 1000 Gems, 10 Dungeon Keys | None |

Using these rewards efficiently is key. Instead of spending all your Gems on basic summons, wait for banners that feature high-tier Legendary units that complement your current team.

<Callout type="success" title="Starter Strategy">
Use the `UPDATE1` and `LAUNCH` codes first. This gives you enough Gems for multiple 10-pulls, significantly increasing your chances of landing at least two Legendary units before you even finish the tutorial missions.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="The Evolution Process: From Legendary to Elite">

Evolution is the most critical mechanic for maintaining the relevance of your units as you progress into World 3. A base Legendary unit will eventually fall off in damage compared to enemies with millions of HP. Evolution fixes this by multiplying base stats and occasionally adding new ability effects.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Collect Duplicates">
    You must have at least one (and often more) identical copy of the unit you wish to evolve. High-tier evolutions require multiple duplicates.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Farm Souls">
    Souls are obtained by selling units of the same rarity. To evolve a Legendary unit, you must sell other Legendaries to acquire "Legendary Souls."
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Acquire Evolution Stones">
    These are rare drops found in high-level Dungeons and Raids. Use your Dungeon Keys from codes to farm these early.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Complete the Ritual">
    Visit the Evolution NPC in the main hub. Ensure your main unit is unequipped before attempting the process.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Callout type="warning" title="Resource Warning">
Selling units for Souls is permanent. Only sell Legendary units that you have in excess and do not plan on using in your main squad. Never sell a Mythic unit unless you are absolutely certain you have enough duplicates for your final team build.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Tower Mode and Slot Optimization">

Even the strongest **anime tactical simulator legendary units** are useless if you can't deploy them. By default, your deployment slots are heavily restricted. Tower Mode is the only way to permanently increase the number of units you can bring into battle without spending Robux.

| Floor Milestone | Reward | Priority |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Floor 10** | +1 Unit Slot | **Critical** |
| **Floor 25** | +1 Unit Slot | **Critical** |
| **Floor 50** | +1 Unit Slot, Rare Items | **High** |
| **Floor 75** | Exclusive Unit, Gems | **Medium** |
| **Floor 100** | +1 Unit Slot, Secret Unit | **Critical** |

Each additional slot represents a massive jump in total team DPS. If you find yourself stuck on a story mission, stop grinding Yen and focus on pushing at least 10 floors in the Tower.

<Callout type="info" title="Tower Mechanics">
Tower floors do not reset. Once you earn a slot, it is permanent across all worlds. This makes Tower progression the most valuable time investment for any player in 2026.
</Callout>

<Checklist
  id="tower-prep"
  title="Tower Push Checklist:"
  items={[
    "Equip your highest level Legendary units",
    "Bring at least one support unit for buffs",
    "Use Damage Potions for floors 25 and 50",
    "Ensure all units have at least level 1 Talents",
    "Set VFX to 'Off' to prevent lag on higher floors"
  ]}
/>

</Section>

<Section icon={Map} title="World Progression and Raid Farming">

As you move from World 1 to World 3, the enemy HP scales exponentially. Your strategy must shift from pure damage to a balance of traits, talents, and elemental advantages.

**World 1 (Starter)**: Focus on clearing quests quickly. Don't waste time grinding bosses here. Use your initial Legendary units to speedrun to the next area.

**World 2 (Mid-Game)**: This is where you begin farming Raids. Raids provide **Stat Cubes** and **Medals**, which are required to upgrade Talents. Talents are account-wide buffs that make every unit in your inventory stronger.

**World 3+ (End-Game)**: At this stage, you should have a full team of evolved Legendary or Mythic units. Enemies here have massive health pools, making "Nuke" units and high-percentage damage dealers essential.

| Resource | Source | Primary Use |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Yen** | Story Stages | Unlocking new Worlds |
| **Gems** | Tasks/Codes | Summoning new units |
| **Stat Cubes** | Raids | Upgrading Unit Talents |
| **Medals** | Raids | Purchasing exclusive items |
| **Souls** | Selling Units | Unit Evolution |

<Callout type="success" title="Efficiency Tip">
Don't enter Raids until you have at least 3-4 Mythic units or highly evolved Legendaries at level 50+. Rushing Raids too early is inefficient and will result in failed runs.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Premium Boosts: Gamepass Tier List">

For players looking to maximize their efficiency, certain gamepasses offer permanent advantages that significantly speed up the acquisition of **anime tactical simulator legendary units**. While the game is fully playable as a free-to-play (F2P) user, these upgrades provide the best value for your Gems or Robux.

**S-Tier (Must Buy)**:
- **Card Access**: Allows you to open unit cards from anywhere in the world, saving massive amounts of travel time.
- **+2 Units Equip**: Instantly increases your power by allowing more units on the field.
- **+3 Cards Open**: Dramatically speeds up the summoning process when hunting for specific Legendaries.

**A-Tier (Great Value)**:
- **2x Gold/Yen**: Essential for unlocking later worlds quickly.
- **2x EXP**: Helps your units reach level 100+ much faster.

<Callout type="tip" title="F2P Alternative">
You can purchase many of these gamepasses using Gems earned in-game. Save your Gems from the `UPDATE1` code if you prefer utility over immediate summons.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "What are the best anime tactical simulator legendary units to keep?",
    answer: "You should keep any Legendary unit that has a 'Nuke' ability or high Area of Effect (AoE) damage. Units like Robin or Subaru (if available in current banners) are historically top-tier due to their massive utility and damage scaling."
  },
  {
    question: "How do I get more unit slots without spending Robux?",
    answer: "The best way is to progress through Tower Mode. Reaching floors 10, 25, 50, and 100 each grants an additional permanent unit slot, allowing for a much stronger team composition."
  },
  {
    question: "Should I focus on Traits or Talents first?",
    answer: "Focus on Talents first. Talents are easier to upgrade using Stat Cubes from Raids and provide more consistent power increases. Traits require Reroll tokens, which are better saved for your final, evolved Mythic units."
  },
  {
    question: "Why is my damage so low in World 3?",
    answer: "World 3 features a massive HP jump. If your damage feels low, check if your units are evolved, ensure their levels are near the cap, and make sure you have unlocked all possible unit slots from the Tower."
  }
]} />

</Section>
